question_text: Which of the following best describes what happens during a reversible chemical change?

options.label: A
options.text: New substances are formed and can be converted back to the original

options.label: B
options.text: Only the appearance changes but the particles stay the same

options.label: C
options.text: The material is destroyed and cannot be recovered

options.label: D
options.text: The state of matter changes from solid to liquid

explanation: A reversible chemical change, like dissolving sugar in water, forms new combinations of particles that can be reversed (by evaporating the water) to recover the original substance. This is rare but important to distinguish from irreversible changes.
